body.title: Granny: dispatched_cycle.json accumulates stale IDs, blocks re-dispatch of bounced tickets
body.intention: I intend that Granny re-dispatches tickets that bounce from in_progress back to sprint.
body.description:
dispatched_cycle.json persists ticket IDs across Granny restarts. When a ticket bounces from in_progress back to sprint (e.g. after a fake-DONE reset or a swallowed send-keys recovery), Granny sees it in the dispatched set and skips it permanently. Observed: all reopened fake-DONE tickets (T-web-ui-circuit-breakers etc.) were in the dispatched set and required manual pruning before Granny would pick them up.

Fix: when a ticket's status is sprint AND its ID is in dispatched_cycle.json, remove it from the set (let Granny re-evaluate). The simplest implementation: in run_once(), before the `if tid in dispatched: continue` check, verify the ticket's current status is not already done/closed. If it's back to sprint, remove from dispatched and proceed.

**Affected files:** devices/granny/daemon.py (run_once, _load_dispatched)
**Design rules:** external state — dispatched set persists in ~/.granny/dispatched_cycle.json
**Scope boundary:** fix stale-ID accumulation; preserve IDs for done/closed tickets to avoid re-dispatch
**Completion criteria:** ticket that bounces in_progress→sprint is re-dispatched by Granny within one poll cycle without manual dispatched_cycle.json editing
